title: BUEM GeoJSON Request Schema v3
description: Request schema for the BUEM building energy model. Each feature represents one building. The buem object contains two concerns: building (complete physical description — classification, geometry, and thermal performance) and solver (execution settings). All measurable quantities carry an explicit unit field. SI units are assumed when unit is omitted. Dimensionless ratios use unit: '-'.

features.description: One entry per building to simulate

coordinates.description: [longitude, latitude, elevation?] — authoritative building location, used for weather station lookup

buem.description: BUEM model input. building contains the complete physical description of the building. solver contains execution settings.

building.description: Complete physical description of the building. Classification fields (type, country, etc.) sit at this level. Geometry and per-element thermal properties are in envelope. Building-wide thermal parameters are in thermal.

A_ref.description: Reference floor area. Derived from floor element areas if omitted.

building_type.description: Building type code (e.g. SFH single-family, MFH multi-family, AB apartment block, TH terraced house)

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code (e.g. NL, DE)

attic_condition.description: Attic conditioning status: - not applicable, N not conditioned, P partially conditioned, C conditioned, NI not insulated, PI partially insulated

cellar_condition.description: Cellar conditioning status — same codes as attic_condition

neighbour_status.description: Attached neighbours: B_Alone detached, B_N1 one shared wall, B_N2 two shared walls

envelope.description: All building surfaces. Each element carries both its geometry (area, orientation, tilt) and its thermal properties (U-value, solar gain coefficient, etc.) in a single entry. No separate thermal properties list is needed.

elements.description: List of all building surfaces. The id must be unique within the feature.

envelope_element.description: A single building surface. Geometry and thermal properties are defined together. For ventilation elements, no surface geometry is needed — only air_changes.

id.description: User-defined unique identifier for this surface element

parent_id.description: Id of the envelope element this surface is embedded in. Required for windows and doors. A window is embedded in a wall (or roof for a skylight); a door is embedded in a wall.

U.description: Thermal transmittance (U-value). Applies to wall, roof, floor, window, and door elements.

b_transmission.description: Transmission correction factor for elements adjacent to unheated spaces (e.g. cellar, attic). 1.0 = fully exposed to outdoor conditions. TABULA: b_Transmission_*

g_gl.description: Solar heat gain coefficient — window elements only. Fraction of incident solar radiation that enters the building. TABULA: g_gl_n_Window

air_changes.description: Air changes per hour — ventilation elements only

thermal.description: Building-wide thermal performance parameters aligned with IEE TABULA definitions. All fields are optional — model defaults (SI units) apply when omitted. Per-element thermal properties (U-value, g_gl, etc.) are defined directly on each envelope element.

n_air_infiltration.description: Uncontrolled air infiltration rate through gaps and cracks in the building fabric — TABULA: n_air_infiltration

n_air_use.description: Ventilation air change rate during building occupancy — TABULA: n_air_use

c_m.description: Internal thermal capacity per m² of floor area — how much heat the building structure can store. TABULA: c_m

thermal_class.description: ISO 13790 thermal mass class — used to derive c_m when c_m is not provided explicitly

comfortT_lb.description: Heating setpoint — minimum acceptable indoor temperature

comfortT_ub.description: Cooling setpoint — maximum acceptable indoor temperature

design_T_min.description: Outdoor design temperature used for peak heating load calculation — TABULA: Theta_e

F_sh_hor.description: Shading reduction factor for horizontal surfaces (e.g. roof) — TABULA: F_sh_hor

F_sh_vert.description: Shading reduction factor for vertical surfaces (e.g. walls, windows) — TABULA: F_sh_vert

F_f.description: Window frame area fraction — proportion of the window opening that is frame rather than glass. TABULA: F_f

F_w.description: Window non-perpendicularity correction factor — TABULA: F_w

solver.description: Execution settings for the BUEM thermal solver. These control how the model runs, not what it simulates.

use_milp.description: Use Mixed Integer Linear Programming solver (CVXPY + CBC) instead of the default scipy sparse solver. Slower but handles inequality constraints exactly.

parallel_thermal.description: Run heating and cooling simulations in parallel within a single building

use_chunked_processing.description: Process the 8760-hour timeseries in chunks to limit memory usage

measurement.description: Base type for all measurable quantities. The unit field defaults to the SI unit for the quantity type when omitted.
